name: omlx-toolsteer
description: item 29 omlx-fix arm: micro-baseline + ADDITIVE tool-call steering (original mlx-gemma-rules.md + a 'emit real tool calls, never narrate/code them' section appended via rules.content — additive, NOT replacing, per item-19). Targets omlx's dominant residual failure (model narrates tool use instead of calling). Pairs with scripts/omlx_repair_proxy.py (eos-strip + text-tool-call parsing). Sampling/skill/tools identical to micro-baseline.

rules.content:
# Local Gemma coding-agent rules (generated by scripts/mlx.sh opencode-config)

This stack's wall-clock is **prefill-dominated** (~0.4 tok/s cold) and a 16 GB M1
hits a ~40–50K-token Metal-OOM cliff. Keep every turn's prompt small.

## Reading files (always)
- NEVER read a whole file when you only need part of it. Use the `read` tool's
  `offset`/`limit` to read a bounded RANGE of lines.
- Narrow with `grep`/`glob` FIRST, then `read` a small window around the match.
- When grep reports the line a function is on, `read` with `offset` set to THAT
  exact line (do not start several lines above it) and a tight `limit` of 6–8
  lines — a single function is only a few lines, so a limit above 8 over-reads.

For detailed read-paging and edit patterns, load the `coding-discipline` skill.

## Tool use (REQUIRED — emit real tool calls)
To inspect code you MUST invoke the `grep` and `read` tools as actual tool calls.
- NEVER describe or narrate a tool action in prose or brackets. Do NOT write `[grep is used to find ...]`, `[runs grep ...]`, or `[uses read ...]`.
- NEVER write a tool call as text or code. Do NOT write `print(grep(...))`, `grep pattern="..."`, or similar — emit a real tool call instead.
- Each step: either emit a real tool call, or give your final answer once you have read the relevant code.
